channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: A propaganda war on the National Mall pits Trump against satirical statues and posters
item.description: The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration and its critics are waging a war of images on the National Mall like none before. The president's face stares down from federal buildings while statues and posters mock him below.

(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: The oil industry is betting big on plastics. Here's what that means for the future
item.description: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.content:encoded: Journalist Beth Gardiner says the fossil fuel industry is increasingly reliant upon plastic products. Her book is Plastic Inc.: The Secret History and Shocking Future of Big Oil's Biggest Bet.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Homesick in a foreign country, a teenager meets a lifelong friend
item.description: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.content:encoded: Holly Deiaco-Smith was feeling homesick while studying abroad in France when she was 19 years old. An encounter at the post office changed everything and led to a decades-long friendship.
item.dc:creator: Autumn Barnes

item.title: From scrappy startup to tech giant, Apple celebrates its 50th year
item.description: Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.
item.content:encoded:
Countercultural mythmaking and global corporate dominance have helped the tech corporation sail through criticism.

(Image credit: Paul Sakuma)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
item.description: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.content:encoded: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: 'The Keeper' is a grand finale to Tana French's Cal Hooper crime series
item.description: Set in a quaint Irish village, The Keeper follows The Searcher and The Hunter, and solidifies the crime series' status as a contemporary classic.
item.content:encoded: Set in a quaint Irish village, The Keeper follows The Searcher and The Hunter, and solidifies the crime series' status as a contemporary classic.
item.dc:creator: Maureen Corrigan

item.title: Inside the high-priced retreats promising to help men reclaim their masculinity
item.description: The Trump era has brought a resurgence of the "alpha male." New Yorker writer Charles Bethea reports on camps where men crawl through mud and sit in ice baths in an effort to reclaim masculinity.
item.content:encoded: The Trump era has brought a resurgence of the "alpha male." New Yorker writer Charles Bethea reports on camps where men crawl through mud and sit in ice baths in an effort to reclaim masculinity.
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Former Alex Jones employee says: 'It was nonsense, it was lies'
item.description: Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.
item.content:encoded:
Josh Owens spent four years as a video editor and field producer for Jones' Infowars media company. "It was all about making things look cinematic," he says. Owens' memoir is The Madness of Believing.

(Image credit: Joe Buglewicz)
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Thieves steal paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse from a private museum in Italy
item.description: Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.
item.content:encoded:
Thieves made off with three paintings by Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse worth millions of euros from a museum near the city of Parma in northern Italy.

(Image credit: Domenico Stinellis)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Farmworker communities are still reeling after Chavez sexual abuse allegations
item.description: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.content:encoded: Cesar Chavez once lived in Delano, home of the United Farm Workers' first HQ. In the wake of sexual abuse allegations against him, many in the city struggle over removing his name from public spaces.
item.dc:creator: Kerry Klein
